mcqs.question: What is the default access specifier for members of a class?
mcqs.explanation: By default, all members of a class are private in C++. This is a key difference from structs where members are public by default.

mcqs.question: Which keyword is used to create a class in C++?
mcqs.explanation: The 'class' keyword is used to define a class in C++. It creates a user-defined data type.

mcqs.question: When is a destructor called automatically?

options.text: When object goes out of scope

mcqs.explanation: Destructors are called when an object goes out of scope or when delete is applied to a pointer pointing to a dynamically allocated object.

mcqs.question: What is the return type of a constructor?
mcqs.explanation: Constructors do not have a return type, not even void. They are called automatically to initialize objects.

mcqs.question: Which pointer points to the current object?
mcqs.explanation: 'this' is a hidden pointer available within member functions that points to the object for which the function was called.

mcqs.question: What is the scope resolution operator used for?

options.text: To define member functions outside class

mcqs.explanation: The :: operator is used to define member functions outside the class and to access static members.

mcqs.question: Can a friend function access private members of a class?
mcqs.explanation: A friend function, though not a member, is granted special access to private and protected members of the class.

mcqs.question: Which constructor is called when an object is created without arguments?
mcqs.explanation: A default constructor is one that can be called with no arguments. It may be compiler-generated or user-defined.

mcqs.question: What is a static data member?
mcqs.explanation: Static data members are shared among all objects of a class and exist even when no objects exist. They are also called class variables.

mcqs.question: What does a const member function guarantee?
mcqs.explanation: A const member function cannot modify data members and can only call other const member functions.

mcqs.question: Which access specifier allows inheritance?
mcqs.explanation: All three access specifiers can be used for inheritance. They determine the visibility of base class members in derived class.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of a copy constructor?

options.text: To copy one object to another

mcqs.correctAnswer: To copy one object to another
mcqs.explanation: Copy constructor initializes a new object as a copy of an existing object. It is called during pass-by-value and return-by-value.

mcqs.question: What happens if user defines any constructor?
mcqs.explanation: If user defines any constructor (except copy constructor), the compiler still generates a default copy constructor.

mcqs.question: Which of these is NOT a special member function?
mcqs.explanation: Display function is a regular member function. Constructor, destructor, and copy constructor are special member functions with specific purposes.

mcqs.question: What is data hiding in OOP?
mcqs.explanation: Data hiding (encapsulation) restricts direct access to certain object components, preventing unintended modifications.

mcqs.question: Can static member functions use 'this' pointer?
mcqs.explanation: Static member functions do not have a 'this' pointer because they belong to the class, not to any specific object.

mcqs.question: What is an object in C++?
mcqs.explanation: An object is a concrete entity created from a class blueprint. It occupies memory and can be manipulated.

mcqs.question: Which operator is used to access members of an object through pointer?
mcqs.explanation: The arrow operator (->) is used to access class members through a pointer to an object. It is equivalent to (*ptr).member.

mcqs.question: What is the size of an empty class in C++?
mcqs.explanation: An empty class in C++ has a size of 1 byte to ensure that each object has a unique address in memory.

mcqs.question: Where are member functions defined inside class body called?
mcqs.explanation: Member functions defined inside the class body are implicitly inline, though the compiler may choose not to inline them.
